commands.review_summary:
The PR skips `agents.defaults.models` keys ending in `/*` while building concrete model-catalog alias metadata.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than an executed failing run: current `buildModelCatalogMetadata` sends wildcard `agents.defaults.models` keys through `resolveAllowlistModelKey` as concrete refs. The existing docs and tests establish `provider/*` as a selector shape.

target.title: fix(agents): skip wildcard catalog metadata refs
target.body:
## Summary

Skips wildcard default model allowlist entries while building concrete model catalog metadata.

Entries such as `openai-codex/*` are valid allowlist selectors, but they are not concrete model ids and do not have per-model alias metadata to resolve. The catalog metadata builder now ignores those wildcard keys instead of sending them through concrete model normalization.

Split out from #85817 so the policy-scoping PR remains policy-only.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-agent-model-catalog-wildcard node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts -- --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000` -> 2 files, 6 tests passed
- `/root/src/openclaw-policy-agent-scoped-design/node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: wildcard default model allowlist entries are treated as selectors, not concrete catalog model ids.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 checkout under `/root/src/openclaw-agent-model-catalog-wildcard`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-agent-model-catalog-wildcard node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts -- --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000`

Evidence after fix: model catalog visibility tests passed in both agent shards, including the wildcard default discovery case.

Observed result after fix: 2 files and 6 tests passed; oxlint and `git diff --check` passed.

What was not tested: live provider catalog discovery.
